methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA represents intentional infrastructure for distributed AI literacy building through community education, signaling potential for peer-to-peer capability development beyond traditional tech hubs if execution reaches beyond academic boundaries.

signals.title: RC-RV partners with ed2go to launch innovative, on-demand workforce training programs - Roanoke College
signals.signal_reading: RC-RV's adoption of ed2go's platform-delivered workforce training suggests shallow, distributed access to AI-adjacent skills in traditionally underserved regions, but the on-demand model risks creating credential consumers rather than capability builders.

signals.title: EdgeCore secures $1.5B toward Sterling data centers construction
signals.signal_reading: EdgeCore's $1.5B investment in Sterling hyperscale data centers deepens NoVA's infrastructure moat as the physical backbone of AI computing, concentrating critical technical expertise and economic benefits while other regions remain dependent consumers of these centralized services.

signals.title: Virginia Tech-backed energy tech hub opens in Newport News
signals.signal_reading: Virginia Tech's energy tech hub opening in Newport News signals intentional capability seeding outside NoVA, but the academic-backed model still concentrates deep expertise in metro-adjacent areas rather than achieving true distributed development across rural Virginia.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: Axios reporting on AI pressure hitting Richmond's young workforce reveals anxiety without corresponding skill-building infrastructure, suggesting shallow awareness of AI disruption spreading faster than deep capability to respond—a classic precursor to the widening gap scenario.
